key_insight: A two-level machine-learning workflow makes it possible to map the thermodynamic stability of complex hybrid perovskite alloys across composition and phase space.
engineering_relevance: The results show that Sn-based (Cs/FA)Sn(Br/I)3 has a narrower stability window than the Pb-based analogue, helping narrow down compositional targets for more stable solar-cell materials.
method: Graph neural network interatomic potentials trained on DFT data for fast structure relaxations
Secondary ML models predict energies directly from unrelaxed alloy structures
Free-energy landscapes computed across compositions and phases while accounting for alloy disorder and FA orientations
evidence_signal: The models are used to generate free-energy landscapes for (Cs/FA)Pb(Br/I)3 and (Cs/FA)Sn(Br/I)3, revealing composition-dependent stability trends and phase regions.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate training data and may be sensitive to coverage of the multicomponent composition space, especially for disordered and orientationally complex configurations.
paper_title: Charting the thermodynamic stability of hybrid perovskite alloys with machine learning
